'''Matty Foster''' (born 25 June 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He spent time on loan from the [[St Helens RLFC|Saints]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
He signed from amateur team [[Blackbrook A.R.L.F.C.|Blackbrook ARLFC]]. He has progressed through the St Helens scholarship programme and represented England U16 as well as the Lancashire Academy, debuting off of the bench against [[Salford Red Devils]] at the back end of the 2020 season.

==Playing career==

===St Helens===
Foster made his first team début for St Helens, coming off the substitute interchange bench against the [[Salford Red Devils]] on 26 Oct 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54675102|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 12-10 St Helens|website=BBC Sport|access-date=27 Oct 2020}}</ref> Due to end-of-season fixture congestion caused by the [[COVID-19 pandemic]], Saints fielded a very young side, resting the majority of first team players, in preparation of their derby match against the [[Wigan Warriors]] just four days later. On 21 September 2023, it was announced that Foster would be departing St Helens at the end of the 2023 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-confirm-11-departures-including-overseas-star-will-hopoate/|title=St Helens confirm 11 departures, including overseas star Will Hopoate|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (loan)===
On 17 March 2021, it was reported that he joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]] on the RFL's new two-week-loan system. This loan was discontinued after Foster suffered a dual jaw break within the squad's first round game against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Mar 2021|accessdate=17 Mar 2021|title=Leigh bring in four loan players from Super League clubs|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-bring-in-four-loan-players-from-super-league-clubs/}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (2nd loan)===
On 4 August 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the Leigh club in the [[Super League]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=St Helens Star|date=4 Aug 2021|accessdate=4 Aug 2021|title=Josh Eaves and Matty Foster join Leigh Centurions on loan|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/19490343.josh-eaves-matty-foster-join-leigh-centurions-loan/}}</ref>

=== Swinton Lions (Dual Registration) ===
During the backend of the [[Betfred Super League|2023 Betfred Super League]] season, it was reported that he had joined [[Swinton Lions]] on a dual-registration basis that had been agreed with current club at the time, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens RLFC]]. Scoring 2 tries across 6 games in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

===Salford Red Devils===
On 1 Nov 2023 it was reported that he will join [[Salford Red Devils]] for 2024<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=1 Nov 2023|title=Matty Foster: Salford Red Devils sign versatile forward after St Helens release|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67287334}}</ref>

===Swinton Lions (loan)===
On 26 Apr 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=26 Apr 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Warrington Wolves and Salford Red Devils forwards make short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/warrington-wolves-and-salford-red-devils-forwards-make-short-term-loan-move/}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 22 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Salford Red Devils|date=22 Aug 2025|accessdate=30 Aug 2025|author=Harry Siddall|title=MATTY FOSTER LEAVES SALFORD TO JOIN OLDHAM|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.com/matty-foster-leaves-salford-to-join-oldham/}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 22 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Super League newcomers sign ex-Salford Red Devils man as length of deal confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-sign-matty-foster-for-2026}}</ref>
